location.origin: Everett, Washington. Kenny Loggins was born January 7, 1948, in Everett -- an industrial port city approximately thirty miles north of Seattle on Interstate 5. The family moved to Southern California when Loggins was young. Everett is the engine's fourth I-5 corridor node after Portland, Seattle, and Bellingham. First 'departure city' -- a location that produces an artist and exports them to a career elsewhere.
location.formative: Alhambra/San Gabriel Valley, Southern California. Loggins grew up in the crucible of 1960s-70s Southern California folk and rock. The Laurel Canyon sound. Session musician culture. Columbia Records. Jim Messina. The career was built entirely in Los Angeles -- studios, labels, movie soundtracks, arena tours. No professional connection to the Pacific Northwest.
location.career: Loggins & Messina (1971-1976, Columbia Records -- seven studio albums, folk-rock with country/jazz inflections, 'Danny's Song,' 'Your Mama Don't Dance'). Solo career (1977-present, Columbia Records -- 'This Is It' Grammy winner 1979, 'I'm Alright' Caddyshack 1980, 'Footloose' #1 Hot 100 1984, 'Danger Zone' Top Gun 1986). The 'King of the Movie Soundtrack.' 20+ albums across five decades. Career based in Los Angeles.

labels: Columbia Records (1971-present: one of the oldest and most storied labels in recorded music history; first Columbia Records artist in the engine; second major label after Atlantic Records at degree 58; provided the recording budgets, studio musicians, distribution, and promotional apparatus for the entire career)

members.name: Kenny Loggins
members.note: Born January 7, 1948, in Everett, Washington. The FM voice -- polished, powerful, radio-ready. Grammy winner. The 'King of the Movie Soundtrack.' Career built entirely in Los Angeles. The third Export in the engine after Quincy Jones (0) and Jimi Hendrix (36). E=3 reflects attenuated local connection, not global commercial success.

associated_musicians.name: Jim Messina
associated_musicians.note: Formerly of Buffalo Springfield and Poco. Producer turned full partner. Seven studio albums on Columbia Records. The folk-rock era before the solo career. Messina brought production chops and band sensibility; Loggins brought the voice and songs.

associated_musicians.name: Death Cab for Cutie
associated_musicians.note: Both PNW-born. Death Cab migrated within the PNW (Bellingham to Seattle, E=4, indie ecosystem, deep local roots). Loggins left entirely (Everett to Los Angeles, E=3, major label, no local roots). The energy gap measures the connection gap.

associated_musicians.name: Pickwick
associated_musicians.note: Pickwick: brief (6 years), local (Capitol Hill), defunct, micro-label (Maker Records), retro-soul voice. Loggins: marathon (55+ years), global (LA), active, major label (Columbia), FM voice. The brief blaze and the FM marathon.

associated_musicians.name: Ann Wilson
associated_musicians.note: Both 1970s-80s arena acts from PNW. Heart and Loggins toured the same circuit. Wilson stayed connected to the PNW (E=5). Loggins left (E=3). The energy gap -- two full levels -- measures the connection gap.

associated_musicians.name: Jimi Hendrix
associated_musicians.note: Both PNW-born, career elsewhere. Hendrix: Seattle to London/New York. Loggins: Everett to Los Angeles. The Export pattern. Third Export after Jones (0) and Hendrix (36).

associated_musicians.name: Quincy Jones
associated_musicians.note: Jones raised in Seattle, career in LA. Loggins born in Everett, career in LA. Both PNW-to-LA exports. Both defined by polished, commercially successful music. The FM sheen connects them.

theory_nodes.concept: The FM sheen (sixty-fourth acoustic element -- the acoustic quality of a voice and production style optimized for radio broadcast; polish, clarity, warmth, absence of rough edges; the sound of investment: expensive studios, professional engineers, session musicians; the opposite of the indie aesthetic's cultivated imperfection; Loggins' voice close-miked, compressed, equalized for maximum clarity; the sound of mainstream commercial music 1970s-1990s)
examples: Kenny Loggins (degree 63) -- the polished, radio-ready voice across five decades. 'This Is It,' 'Footloose,' 'Danger Zone' all demonstrate the FM sheen: every note clean, every phrase articulate, every emotional moment legible to a mass audience.
Contrast with the throwback (degree 62, Pickwick) -- temporal displacement. The FM sheen is the mainstream of its era, not anachronistic but perfectly calibrated for its context.
Contrast with the braided voice (degree 61, Y La Bamba) -- cultural layering. The FM sheen erases cultural specificity, designed for mass-market universalism.
Contrast with the bare wire (degree 60, Gibbard solo) -- stripped to essentials. The FM sheen is layered with production, surrounded by arrangement, embedded in sonic infrastructure.
Contrast with the ache (degree 58, Death Cab) -- emotional roughness. The FM sheen is emotional smoothness: the same emotions through a polished instrument.
The FM sheen is not neutral. It carries the values of mainstream American commercial culture. The polish says: professional, for everyone, not subcultural, not underground.
theory_nodes.description: The FM sheen is the sixty-fourth acoustic element in the Seattle 360 engine. It describes the acoustic quality of a voice and production style optimized for radio broadcast -- characterized by polish, clarity, warmth, and the absence of rough edges. The FM sheen is the sound of mainstream commercial music from the 1970s through the 1990s, the product of expensive studios and professional craft deployed in service of accessibility.

ancestors: The throwback (degree 62, Pickwick) -- the FM sheen is what the throwback displaces. The mainstream sound.
The testimony roar (degree 54) -- power without mediation. The FM sheen is power through maximum mediation.
The bare wire (degree 60, Gibbard solo) -- stripped essentials. The FM sheen is maximum layering.
genealogy.relationship: Sixty-four acoustic elements across sixty-four degrees. The FM sheen is the engine's first mainstream-commercial acoustic element. Track element 65 at degree 64.

theory_nodes.concept: The Everett Departure (sixty-fourth artist-city pattern -- the pattern of an artist born in a city who builds their entire career elsewhere, leaving the birth city as a biographical footnote; Loggins born in Everett, career in Los Angeles; specific instance of the Export pattern; the birth-city-as-footnote; the attenuated connection; opposite of the Bellingham Ascent)
examples: Kenny Loggins (degree 63) -- born in Everett, Washington. Career built entirely in Los Angeles. Everett's contribution: a birth certificate, not a discography.
The Export pattern genealogy: Quincy Jones (degree 0), Jimi Hendrix (degree 36), Kenny Loggins (degree 63). All PNW-born, career elsewhere.
Contrast with the Bellingham Ascent (degree 58, Death Cab) -- migration within the PNW. The Everett Departure migrates out entirely.
Contrast with the brief blaze (degree 62, Pickwick) -- hyperlocal. The Everett Departure has no local footprint at all.
Contrast with the corridor bridge (degree 61, Y La Bamba) -- connects cities. The Everett Departure disconnects from the city of origin.
theory_nodes.description: The Everett Departure is the sixty-fourth artist-city pattern in the Seattle 360 engine. It describes the pattern of an artist born in a city who builds their entire career elsewhere, leaving the birth city as a biographical footnote rather than a cultural base. A specific instance of the Export pattern. The opposite of every locally-rooted pattern in the engine.

ancestors: The brief blaze (degree 62, Pickwick) -- hyperlocal. The Everett Departure is the opposite: no local footprint.
The Bellingham Ascent (degree 58, Death Cab) -- migration within region. The Everett Departure migrates out.
The corridor bridge (degree 61, Y La Bamba) -- connects cities. The Everett Departure disconnects.
genealogy.relationship: Sixty-four artist-city patterns across sixty-four degrees. Third Export pattern (after degree 0 and 36). Track pattern 65 at degree 64.

theory_nodes.concept: Blue note -- the hometown stranger (sixty-third deployment; the blue note that sounds when an artist is claimed by a city they barely know; Loggins is from Everett but not of Everett; the gap between 'from' and 'of'; geographic origin does not equal cultural identity; the connection is genealogical rather than experiential)
examples: Kenny Loggins (degree 63) -- born in Everett, Washington. Did not grow up in Everett's music scene. Did not play Everett's venues. Did not write songs about Everett. The connection is a birth certificate, not a lived relationship.
Contrast with the closed catalog (degree 62, Pickwick) -- silence after dissolution. The hometown stranger is the silence of a connection that never existed.
Contrast with the untranslatable ache (degree 61, Y La Bamba) -- gap between languages. The hometown stranger is the gap between biography and identity.
Contrast with the incomplete catalog (degree 59, Long Winters) -- music that never got made. The hometown stranger is a relationship that never got made.
The blue note is not grief. Loggins did not lose Everett; he never had it. The recognition that 'born in' and 'from' and 'of' are three different claims.
theory_nodes.description: The hometown stranger is the sixty-third blue note deployment in the Seattle 360 engine. It describes the blue note that sounds when an artist is claimed by a city they barely know. Loggins is from Everett but not of Everett. The gap between geographic origin and cultural identity. The hometown stranger walks through a city that claims him as a native and does not recognize a single street.

ancestors: Blue note -- the closed catalog (degree 62, Pickwick): silence after dissolution. The hometown stranger is silence of a connection that never existed.
Blue note -- the untranslatable ache (degree 61, Y La Bamba): gap between languages. The hometown stranger is gap between biography and identity.
All sixty-two prior blue note deployments (degrees 0-62)
genealogy.relationship: Sixty-three deployments across sixty-four degrees. First departure-triggered blue note. Track deployment 64 at degree 64.

theory_nodes.concept: Soft Rock/Folk at Genre Stage 45 -- third consecutive hybrid genre classification; Folk/Indie (61), Indie/Folk (62), Soft Rock/Folk (63); the slash as default; Soft Rock new to engine; the polished FM radio sound of 1970s-80s; Folk element from Loggins & Messina era; career arc from folk-rock to soft rock to movie soundtrack encoded in a single label
examples: Kenny Loggins (degree 63) -- Soft Rock/Folk. The Loggins & Messina era is folk-rock. The solo career is soft rock. The soundtrack work is arena pop. The hybrid brackets a multi-decade career.
Three consecutive hybrids: Folk/Indie (61), Indie/Folk (62), Soft Rock/Folk (63). The slash has become the default.
Genre progression: Folk (57) -> Indie Rock (58) -> Indie Rock (59) -> Folk (60) -> Folk/Indie (61) -> Indie/Folk (62) -> Soft Rock/Folk (63). Three hybrids after the Folk-Indie oscillation.
Soft Rock is new to the engine. The polished, radio-ready sound of 1970s-80s FM radio. Yacht Rock territory. Album-oriented radio format.
theory_nodes.description: Soft Rock/Folk at degree 63 establishes Genre Stage 45, the engine's third consecutive hybrid genre classification. Soft Rock is a new genre element -- the polished FM radio sound of the 1970s-80s that has no precedent in the engine's first sixty-two degrees. The Folk component reflects the Loggins & Messina era. Three consecutive hybrids confirm that genre hybridization is the norm in this section of the engine.

ancestors: Indie/Folk at degree 62 (Pickwick) -- Genre Stage 44. Second hybrid. Soft Rock/Folk introduces a new genre element.
Folk/Indie at degree 61 (Y La Bamba) -- Genre Stage 43. First hybrid. The three-hybrid streak begins here.
Folk at degree 60 (Gibbard solo) -- Genre Stage 42. Pure Folk. Last non-hybrid genre.
genealogy.relationship: Genre Stage 45 at degree 63. Third consecutive hybrid. Soft Rock enters the engine. Track genre at degree 64.

theory_nodes.concept: E=3 as 'connected but not embedded' -- E=3 back-to-back with Y La Bamba (61); energy distribution 2:20:15:16:10:2; E=3 reflects attenuated local connection not global commercial success; E=3 is the engine's energy for artists with ambiguous or attenuated local ties; the contrast with E=5 (Ann Wilson) measures connection not volume
examples: Kenny Loggins (degree 63) -- E=3. Massive commercial success (Danger Zone, Footloose, Grammy winner) but career built in Los Angeles, not the PNW. E=3 reflects local departure.
E=3 rises to 15 deployments. Distribution after degree 63: E1:2, E2:20, E3:15, E4:16, E5:10, E6:2. E=3 closes gap with E=4 (15 vs 16).

Y La Bamba (61, E=3) was a corridor artist, not fully rooted in one city. Loggins (63, E=3) is a departure artist, not rooted in the region at all. E=3 is becoming the 'connected but not embedded' energy level.
Energy trajectory: E=2 (55-57), E=4 (58-59), E=2 (60), E=3 (61), E=4 (62), E=3 (63). Oscillation with E=3 marking attenuated connections.
Contrast: Ann Wilson (43, E=5) stayed connected to the PNW. Loggins (63, E=3) left. The two-level energy gap measures the connection gap.
theory_nodes.description: E=3 at degree 63 is the fifteenth E=3 deployment in the Seattle 360 engine. Energy distribution: E1:2, E2:20, E3:15, E4:16, E5:10, E6:2. E=3 closes the gap with E=4 (15 vs 16). The E=3 assignment for an artist with massive global commercial success reveals the engine's calibration principle: energy measures local cultural footprint, not commercial volume.

ancestors: E=3 at degree 61 (Y La Bamba): 14th deployment. Corridor artist. E=3 continues as 'connected but not embedded.'
E=4 at degree 62 (Pickwick): 16th deployment. Local, embedded energy. E=3 steps down from E=4.
genealogy.relationship: Fifteen E=3 deployments across sixty-four degrees. E=3 closing gap with E=4 (15 vs 16). Track at degree 64.

theory_nodes.concept: Everett enters -- fourth I-5 corridor node after Portland, Seattle, Bellingham; industrial port city 30 miles north of Seattle; Boeing, Navy, timber; first 'departure city' in the engine; birth city not career city; the I-5 corridor filling in
examples: Everett (degree 63) -- Kenny Loggins born here 1948. Fourth I-5 node. Thirty miles north of Seattle. Boeing, Navy, timber town.
I-5 corridor: Portland (61, Y La Bamba), Seattle (multiple), Everett (63, Loggins), Bellingham (58, Death Cab). Four nodes mapping the Puget Sound urban corridor.
Everett is the engine's first 'departure city' -- a location that produces an artist and exports them elsewhere. Unlike Capitol Hill (workspace) or Bellingham (launchpad).
The geographic pattern is tightening: the engine is mapping the entire I-5 corridor, not just Seattle proper.
theory_nodes.description: Everett enters the engine at degree 63 as the fourth I-5 corridor node. An industrial port city thirty miles north of Seattle, built around Boeing, the Navy, and timber. Everett is the engine's first 'departure city' -- a location that produces an artist and exports them to a career elsewhere. The I-5 corridor now spans from Portland to Bellingham with four mapped nodes.

ancestors: Bellingham at degree 58 (Death Cab) -- second city north of Seattle. Everett fills in between.
Portland at degree 61 (Y La Bamba) -- southern node. Everett extends the northern corridor.
Capitol Hill (multiple degrees) -- workspace city. Everett is departure city.
genealogy.relationship: Fourth I-5 corridor node. First departure city. Track geography at degree 64.

theory_nodes.concept: Columbia Records -- first appearance in engine; second major label after Atlantic (58); one of the oldest labels in recorded music (founded 1887); provided recording budgets, studio musicians, distribution, and promotional apparatus; after micro-label Maker Records (62), the engine swings to opposite extreme
examples: Columbia Records (degree 63) -- Kenny Loggins' label for the entire career from Loggins & Messina (1971) through solo work.
Label scale swing: Maker Records (degree 62, micro-label) to Columbia Records (degree 63, multinational institution). The widest label-scale contrast in the engine.
Label progression: Sub Pop, Barsuk, Kill Rock Stars, self-released, TLE, Maker Records, Columbia Records. From hyperlocal to global.
Columbia provided the movie soundtrack pipeline: label relationships with film studios enabled the Loggins-to-celluloid connection.
theory_nodes.description: Columbia Records enters the engine at degree 63 as the first Columbia Records artist and only the second major-label artist after Atlantic Records (degree 58). Founded in 1887, Columbia is one of the oldest labels in recorded music. The label spectrum in the engine now runs from the hyperlocal micro-label (Maker Records) to the multinational corporate institution (Columbia Records).

ancestors: Atlantic Records at degree 58 (Death Cab) -- first major label. Columbia is the second.
Maker Records at degree 62 (Pickwick) -- micro-label. Columbia is the opposite extreme.
genealogy.relationship: First Columbia Records degree. Second major label in engine. Widest label-scale range. Track label at degree 64.

theory_nodes.concept: The Export pattern -- third Export: Quincy Jones (0), Jimi Hendrix (36), Kenny Loggins (63); PNW-born, career elsewhere; the region produces the artist, the artist produces the career somewhere else; all three ended up in Los Angeles or global circuits; the engine's oldest pattern continues
examples: Three Exports: Quincy Jones (degree 0, raised in Seattle, career in LA), Jimi Hendrix (degree 36, born in Seattle, career London/NY), Kenny Loggins (degree 63, born in Everett, career LA).
The Export is the engine's oldest pattern, established at degree 0. It recurs at degree 36 and now degree 63. The spacing: 0, 36, 63.
Contrast with Death Cab (degree 58) -- migrated within the PNW. The Exports leave the region entirely.
All three Exports are commercially and culturally significant at the national/global level. The PNW produced talent for the world.
Jones and Loggins both ended up in Los Angeles. Both defined by polished, commercially successful music. The FM sheen connects them.
theory_nodes.description: The Export pattern at degree 63 marks its third appearance in the Seattle 360 engine. All three Exports -- Quincy Jones (0), Jimi Hendrix (36), Kenny Loggins (63) -- were born in or raised in the Pacific Northwest and built their careers elsewhere. The pattern says something about the PNW's relationship to its own cultural products: the region incubates talent and then watches it leave.

ancestors: Quincy Jones at degree 0 -- the original Export. PNW to Los Angeles.
Jimi Hendrix at degree 36 -- second Export. Seattle to London/New York.
genealogy.relationship: Third Export in the engine. Spacing: 0, 36, 63. The engine's oldest recurring pattern. Track at future degrees.

theory_nodes.concept: The soundtrack king -- Loggins' career uniquely defined by movie soundtracks; 'I'm Alright' (Caddyshack 1980), 'Footloose' (#1, 1984), 'Danger Zone' (Top Gun 1986); no other engine artist has this relationship with film; the soundtrack became the text, the albums the footnotes; the voice synonymous with cinematic moments
examples: The three defining songs: 'I'm Alright' (Caddyshack), 'Footloose' (#1 Hot 100), 'Danger Zone' (Top Gun). All movie themes.
Remove the movies and Loggins is a respected soft-rock singer-songwriter. With the movies, Loggins is a cultural institution.
'Danger Zone' is more famous than any Loggins album. The soundtrack became the text; the albums became the footnotes.
The voice became synonymous with a specific cinematic moment: the triumphant montage, the training sequence, the emotional climax.
No other engine artist has this total identification with cinema. The relationship is unique.
theory_nodes.description: The soundtrack king phenomenon at degree 63 documents a career uniquely defined by the relationship between music and cinema. Loggins' three defining songs are all movie themes. The soundtrack placements transformed Loggins from a respected soft-rock musician into a cultural institution. The voice became synonymous with cinematic spectacle. No other engine artist holds this relationship with film.

ancestors: No direct ancestor in the engine. The soundtrack king is a unique phenomenon.
The FM sheen (degree 63) enables the soundtrack placements -- the polished, radio-ready voice is also a cinema-ready voice.
genealogy.relationship: First soundtrack-identified artist in the engine. Unique phenomenon. Track whether future degrees include soundtrack-defined artists.

theory_nodes.concept: Gender: male continues -- second consecutive male after degree 62; ratio 18:46 (~28.1%); non-male percentage declining in this section; male three-peat (58-60), non-male (61), male two-peat (62-63); no sustained non-male runs since three-peat at 55-57
examples: Kenny Loggins (degree 63) -- male-identified. After Galen Disston (degree 62, male).
Gender ratio after degree 63: 18:46 (non-male:male), ~28.1%. Dips from 28.6% at degree 62.
Gender pattern: non-male three-peat (55-57), male three-peat (58-60), non-male (61), male two-peat (62-63). Non-male representation declining.
No sustained non-male runs since the three-peat at degrees 55-57.
theory_nodes.description: Kenny Loggins at degree 63 is a male-identified artist, the second consecutive male after degree 62. Gender ratio: 18:46 (~28.1%). The non-male percentage continues to decline from its local peak at degree 57. The gender pattern shows a male lean in this section of the engine.

ancestors: Male at degree 62 (Pickwick): Disston. Male two-peat at 62-63.
Non-male at degree 61 (Y La Bamba): Mendoza. Single non-male degree between male runs.
genealogy.relationship: Gender ratio 18:46 (~28.1%) at degree 63. Male two-peat at 62-63. Track at degree 64.

theory_nodes.concept: Earliest active era in engine -- 1971-present; Loggins predates all folk-zone artists; Loggins & Messina debuted before most engine artists were born; the longest career (55+ years); career-duration spectrum: Pickwick 6 years, Death Cab 27+ years, Loggins 55+ years
examples: 1971-present. Fifty-five years and counting. Predates: Gibson (2006), Death Cab (1997), Long Winters (2001), Gibbard solo (2010), Y La Bamba (2007), Pickwick (2010).
Loggins & Messina debut Sittin' In (1971) -- 26 years before Death Cab's formation.
Career-duration spectrum widens: Pickwick 6 years, Death Cab 27+ years, Loggins 55+ years. Nearly a full order of magnitude.
The brief blaze (62) and the FM marathon (63). Consecutive degrees, maximum duration contrast.
theory_nodes.description: Degree 63 introduces the engine's earliest active era: 1971 to the present. Loggins has been recording for fifty-five years, predating every other folk-zone artist by decades. The career-duration spectrum now spans from Pickwick's six years to Loggins' fifty-five -- nearly a full order of magnitude.

ancestors: Pickwick at degree 62 -- 6-year career. Loggins extends the other end of the spectrum.
Death Cab at degree 58 -- 27+ year career. Loggins doubles it.
genealogy.relationship: Earliest active era. Longest career. Track duration patterns at future degrees.

theory_nodes.concept: Loggins & Messina -- the duo era 1971-1976; Jim Messina (Buffalo Springfield, Poco); seven studio albums on Columbia Records; folk-rock with country and jazz inflections; 'Danny's Song,' 'Your Mama Don't Dance,' 'House at Pooh Corner'; the Folk in Soft Rock/Folk comes from this era
examples: Loggins & Messina (1971-1976) -- seven studio albums on Columbia. Folk-rock with country and jazz inflections.
'Danny's Song' (later hit for Anne Murray), 'Your Mama Don't Dance' (#4 Hot 100), 'House at Pooh Corner.' The warm, acoustic beginning.
The Folk element in Soft Rock/Folk comes from this era. The career arc: folk-rock (duo) -> soft rock (solo) -> movie soundtrack (peak).
Messina: formerly Buffalo Springfield and Poco. Producer turned partner. The collaboration that launched Loggins' career.
theory_nodes.description: The Loggins & Messina era (1971-1976) represents the folk-rock foundation of Kenny Loggins' career. Seven studio albums on Columbia Records with Jim Messina (formerly of Buffalo Springfield and Poco). The duo's sound -- warm, acoustic, folk-rock with country and jazz inflections -- provides the 'Folk' component of the Soft Rock/Folk genre classification.

ancestors: Buffalo Springfield and Poco -- Messina's prior bands. The folk-rock lineage.
Columbia Records -- label from the beginning. The institutional support.
genealogy.relationship: The duo era precedes the solo career by six years. The folk-rock foundation under the soft-rock polish. Track at future degrees.

theory_nodes.concept: Territorial acknowledgment extended: Everett / Snohomish territory -- Snohomish people, Coast Salish cultural area; Everett at confluence of Snohomish River and Possession Sound; Tulalip Reservation north of Everett; engine geographic scope extends northward from Seattle (Duwamish) to Everett (Snohomish)
examples: Everett: Snohomish territory. Coast Salish cultural area. The Snohomish are a federally recognized tribe, now based on the Tulalip Reservation.
Engine territorial scope extends: Seattle/Duwamish (degree 20), Portland/Multnomah (57), Bellingham/Lummi-Nooksack (58), Everett/Snohomish (63).
Loggins born on Snohomish territory, career on Tongva territory (Los Angeles). The territorial acknowledgment follows the artist across territories.
The territorial acknowledgment at degree 63 is the engine's most geographically mobile: tracking an artist across territories rather than anchoring in one.
theory_nodes.description: The territorial acknowledgment at degree 63 extends the engine's geographic scope to Everett and Snohomish territory. The Snohomish people, part of the Coast Salish cultural area, are a federally recognized tribe now based on the Tulalip Reservation. The territorial map now includes four distinct territories: Duwamish (Seattle), Multnomah (Portland), Lummi/Nooksack (Bellingham), and Snohomish (Everett).

ancestors: Territorial acknowledgment at degree 20 (Seattle/Duwamish) -- established.
Extended at degree 57 (Portland/Multnomah).
Extended at degree 58 (Bellingham/Lummi, Nooksack).
Extended at degree 61 (Willamette Valley/Kalapuya).
genealogy.relationship: Territorial acknowledgment extended to Everett/Snohomish at degree 63. Fourth distinct territory. Applied to all degrees.

theory_nodes.concept: The FM marathon vs the brief blaze -- degree 62-63 as maximum duration contrast; Pickwick 6 years/2 releases/defunct vs Loggins 55+ years/20+ albums/active; same section of the engine, opposite temporal profiles; the brief blaze burns bright and ends, the FM marathon burns steady and continues
examples: Pickwick (62): 6 years, 2 releases, defunct, micro-label, Capitol Hill. Loggins (63): 55+ years, 20+ albums, active, major label, Los Angeles.
Consecutive degrees, maximum contrast. Every parameter is inverted.
The throwback (62) and the FM sheen (63). The retro-soul voice and the radio-ready voice. Two consecutive vocal aesthetics at opposite poles.
Maker Records (62) and Columbia Records (63). Micro-label and multinational. The widest label-scale contrast in the engine.
theory_nodes.description: Degrees 62-63 present the engine's maximum duration contrast: Pickwick's six-year brief blaze against Loggins' fifty-five-year FM marathon. Every parameter is inverted across the two consecutive degrees -- duration, scope, label scale, vocal aesthetic, geographic footprint. The brief blaze and the FM marathon are the engine's temporal poles.

ancestors: The brief blaze at degree 62 (Pickwick) -- the short end. The FM marathon is the long end.
The longevity contrast at degree 62 (Pickwick vs Death Cab) -- extended by Loggins to maximum range.
genealogy.relationship: Maximum duration contrast across consecutive degrees. Track temporal patterns at future degrees.

cross_references.relationship: Both PNW-born, opposite trajectories. Death Cab migrated within PNW (Bellingham to Seattle, E=4, indie ecosystem). Loggins left entirely (Everett to LA, E=3, major label). The energy gap measures the connection gap.

Consecutive degrees, maximum contrast. Pickwick: brief (6 years), local, defunct, micro-label, throwback. Loggins: marathon (55+ years), global, active, major label, FM sheen. The brief blaze and the FM marathon.
Both 1970s-80s arena acts from PNW. Wilson stayed connected (E=5). Loggins left (E=3). Two-level energy gap measures the connection gap. Same era, same scale, different relationship to the region.
Export pattern. Both PNW-born, career elsewhere. Hendrix: Seattle to London/NY. Loggins: Everett to LA. Third Export after Jones (0) and Hendrix (36).
Both Exports, both Los Angeles. Both defined by polished, commercially successful music. Both more famous for LA work than PNW origins. The FM sheen connects them.
